title: Praj Demonstrates Integrated Ethanol-to-Jet Pathway for Sustainable Aviation on Fuel with Axens Jetanolâ„¢ Technology
content: Praj Industries has successfully demonstrated its Ethanol to Jet process utilizing Axens Jetanol technology at its fully integrated Sustainable Aviation Fuel demonstration plant at Praj Matrix, its R&D centre, marking a significant milestone in the global transition to low carbon aviation fuels.  The announcement was made at Wings India 2026 in the presence of key industry stakeholders from the SAF ecosystem.  The integrated SAF demo plant, earlier inaugurated by the Honorable Union Minister for Petroleum and Natural Gas, Shri Hardeep Singh Puri, has now proven its technological readiness for both Alcohol to Jet pathways.  After successful demonstration on bio isobutanol as the feedstock, the plant has validated bio ethanol as a feedstock under the Ethanol to Jet pathway, strengthening the versatility and robustness of Prajâ€™s feedstock to SAF technology portfolio.  Axens has licensed to Praj its Jetanol Alcohol to Jet technology, including dehydration, oligomerization and hydrogenation steps, along with catalysts and technical support, as part of the collaboration to advance SAF through the Alcohol to Jet route.  This breakthrough positions Praj as the first company globally to offer a fully integrated, end to end technology and engineering solution for the Alcohol to Jet pathway encompassing both isobutanol and ethanol, validated at demo plant scale and ready for commercial deployment by integrating Axens proven technology with Prajâ€™s technology, engineering and project execution capabilities.  The development enables conversion of ethanol into drop in SAF compliant to ASTM D7566 that is fully compatible with existing aircraft engines and aviation infrastructure, furthering the global transition to low carbon aviation.  Praj has previously supported Indiaâ€™s first commercial passenger flight powered by SAF blended aviation turbine fuel using indigenously produced SAF in collaboration with Indian Oil.
author: Analysts Account
